description: Whether the provider supports OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote MCP servers, including token storage and automatic refresh.

amp.mechanism: oauth_support: Amp supports OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote MCP servers

claude-code.mechanism: mcp_oauth_authentication: OAuth 2.0 for HTTP MCP servers; dynamic client registration; token storage in macOS keychain or credentials file; auto-refresh

cline.mechanism: Cline MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote servers

codex.mechanism: mcp_oauth_support: Codex supports OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote MCP servers

copilot-cli.mechanism: Copilot CLI MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication

crush.mechanism: Crush MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote servers

cursor.mechanism: Cursor's MCP documentation does not describe OAuth 2.0 flows for remote servers.

factory-droid.mechanism: Factory Droid MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication

gemini-cli.mechanism: Gemini CLI MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote servers

kiro.mechanism: Kiro MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication

opencode.mechanism: No OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote MCP servers documented

roo-code.mechanism: No OAuth 2.0 authentication flow documented for Roo Code MCP servers

windsurf.mechanism: Windsurf MCP does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication for remote servers

zed.mechanism: Zed MCP configuration does not document OAuth 2.0 authentication for MCP servers
